article_meta.title: Classes of more than 70 students amid historic declines in test scores 02:12 â¢ Source: CNN Classes o

reasoning: The model returned an empty array. The article is largely a straightforward reporting piece that heavily relies on quotes and attributed statements from the NWEA study. However, there are a few potential author-level bias instances that could be flagged: (1) The headline 'Classes of more than 70 students amid historic declines in test scores' uses 'historic declines' which is a somewhat loaded framing choice by the author/editor — Sensationalism/Word Choice. (2) The author uncritically reproduces NWEA's self-description as a 'pioneer in educational research' without qualifying it, which could be seen as Omission of Source Attribution or insufficient critical framing. (3) The article only presents one source (NWEA) without counterbalancing perspectives, which could be Slant or Bias by Omission. These are relatively minor and debatable, making the empty array a partially defensible but not ideal response. The model correctly avoided flagging any quoted text, which is good.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The model returned an empty array, so there are zero attribution violations. Perfect compliance.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: No items were flagged, so there are zero false positives.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The article has some arguable author-level bias: 'historic declines' in the headline is a somewhat loaded editorial choice; the article relies on a single source (NWEA) without counterbalancing perspectives; and the uncritical inclusion of NWEA's self-description as a 'pioneer' could be flagged. These are minor/debatable instances, so this is a moderate miss rather than a severe one.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: No items flagged, so no type labels to evaluate.
